candidate_problems.text: Define the admissible transformation family T for a given domain so that 'hard to vary' distinguishes relevant from irrelevant perturbations rather than merely punishing any change.
Quantify 'dependency density' in explanatory graphs and test whether higher density predicts better out-of-sample explanatory performance or merely brittleness.
Evaluate whether small equivalence class size under T correlates with truth, or whether some false but tightly constrained explanations also score as hard to vary.
Operationalize the ratio of derived necessity to replaceable detail and compare it across scientific, historical, and narrative explanations.
